data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/10/25 вҖ“ Futures Decline amid Trump's Recession Remarks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Monday morning due to concerns over an economic slowdown. In a Fox News interview on Sunday, President Donald Trump refused to say whether the economy is facing a recession or rising inflation. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 1.03%, 0.63%, and 0.83%, respectively, at 4:42 a.m. EST, March 10.

The previous week saw significant declines across major indices. The Dow Jones, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq Composite fell by 2.37%, 3.1%, and 3.45%, respectively. The downside was attributed to investor concerns about changing trade policies and rising global trade tensions.

Moreover, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments on Friday, indicating no immediate plans for interest rate cuts, added to market uncertainty. The ongoing trade disputes and policy changes continue to impact the economic outlook.

This week, several economic data releases are expected to influence market sentiment. Key releases include January's Job Openings data and the New York Fed survey of consumer expectations on Monday.

Importantly, inflation data will be a primary focus this week, with the February Consumer Price Index (CPI) scheduled for Wednesday and the Producer Price Index (PPI) on Thursday. Further, the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment reading on Friday.

Investors are now focused on upcoming corporate earnings reports from companies such as Oracle (ORCL), Dick’s Sporting Goods (DICK), UiPath (PATH), DocuSign (DOCU), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Dollar General (DG), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Li Auto (LI), and Adobe Systems (ADBE).

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.263%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending lower, hovering near $66.92 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened higher today, rebounding from the previous session’s losses as confidence among investors grew.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Monday

Most of the Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today. Concerns over China’s slowing inflation and weak domestic demand, coupled with heightened trade tensions, have added to market volatility.  

Notably, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was down 1.85%. Also, China's Shenzhen Component and Shanghai Composite indices declined 0.17% and 0.19%, respectively. Further, Japan's Topix index closed lower by 0.29%, while Nikkei index gained 0.38%.

Interested in more economic insights?вҖҜTune in to our LIVE webinar. 

Disclosure
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Adobe (ADBE) Is About to Report Its Earnings This Week. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Creative software developer Adobe (ADBE)is set to release its first quarter Fiscal 2025 financials on March 12. ADBE stock has lost over 19% in the past year, primarily due to uncertainty around monetizing its AI products, rising competition in the creative software market, and tough economic conditions hurting its Digital Media business. Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $4.97 per share for Q1, up 11% year-over-year.

Meanwhile,ГӮВ analysts expect revenues to increase 9.3% from the year-ago figure to $5.66 billion, according to TipRanksГўВҖВҷ data. Notably, Adobe has exceeded EPS estimates for the past nine consecutive quarters.

AnalystsГўВҖВҷ Opinions Ahead of AdobeГўВҖВҷs Q1 Earnings

Ahead of AdobeГўВҖВҷs Q1 earnings, TD Cowen analyst J. Derrick Wood kept a Hold rating on the stock with a price target of $550 per share. Wood noted that though the recent survey shows better partner execution and growth in AdobeГўВҖВҷs small business segment, the outlook for 2025 remains weak.

For Q1 FY25, the analyst expects Adobe to meet or slightly beat first-quarter targets. However, they believe future guidance will remain cautious due to economic uncertainty.  

Meanwhile, RBC Capital analyst Matthew Swanson lowered Adobe’s price target to $550 from $590, while keeping an Outperform rating. Swanson expects the company to report strong first-quarter results on March 12, with the Adobe Summit taking place the following week. He expects Adobe to share more details on AI-related revenue and the growth of Adobe Express, a tool for creating graphics, videos, and web pages, to support Creative Cloud.

Options Traders Anticipate a Large Move

Using TipRanksГўВҖВҷ Options tool, we can see what options traders are expecting from the stock immediately after its earnings report. The expected earnings move is determined by calculating the at-the-money straddle of the options closest to expiration after the earnings announcement. If this sounds complicated, donГўВҖВҷt worry, the Options tool does this for you.

Indeed, it currently says thatГӮВ options traders are expecting a 8.41% moveГӮВ in either direction.

Is Adobe a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, Adobe has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 19 Buys, eight Holds and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $572.17, theГӮВ average ADBE price target implies 27.32% upside potential.

data.title: Top Analyst Downgrades BigBear.ai Stock (BBAI) Rating but Lifts Price Target
data.description:
BigBear.ai (BBAI) stockГўВҖВҷs rating was recently downgraded by Northland Securities analyst Michael Latimore to Hold from Buy. Despite the downgrade, the Top analyst raised the price target to $4 (19.8% upside potential from $2.5. This move reflects the analyst’s reaction over both the challenges BBAI is currently facing and future growth potential. BBAI stock dropped 20.5% in FridayГўВҖВҷs regular trading session.

Importantly, the move came after the company reported lower-than-expected fourth-quarter results. Further, BBAI provided a revenue forecast of $160 million to $180 million for 2025, which came below the analyst estimates of $193.9 million.

Key Reasons Behind the Downgrade

The analyst has cited three major reasons for the rating downgrade. These include a large revenue miss and a slight EBITDA miss in Q4, pointing to weak financial performance. Moreover, Latimore believes that since BBAI relies heavily on government contracts, unpredictable spending patterns could slow growth.

The downgrade also reflects the time required for the new CEO to implement strategic plans and drive meaningful and steady growth.

Factors That Could Boost BigBear.aiГўВҖВҷs Performance

BigBear.ai is making several efforts to boost its performance and regain investor confidence. The company recently improved its financial position. In the fourth quarter, BBAI converted $58 million of debt into equity and reduced net debt to $27 million.

Moreover, BigBear.ai continues to secure major contracts, such as a $165 million production contract with the U.S. Army. These contracts not only drive revenue but also strengthen the companyГўВҖВҷs foothold in the defense and intelligence sectors.

Lastly, by expanding into commercial sectors, BigBear.ai aims to tap higher-margin opportunities, reducing reliance on government contracts.

Is BBAI Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, BBAI stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based onГӮВ two Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. At $5.33, theГӮВ average BigBear.ai price targetГӮВ implies 59.58% upside potential.

See more BBAI analyst ratings.

Disclosure

data.title: U.S. Considers DeepSeek Ban on Government Devices Over Data Privacy Fears
data.description:
The U.S. government is considering banning the Chinese AI app DeepSeek from government devices due to national security concerns, according to the Wall Street Journal. This move adds to growing worries about foreign tech handling sensitive data.

Why DeepSeek Is a Concern

DeepSeek, launched in January 2025, quickly became popular for its advanced AI tools. But it also caught the eye of U.S. lawmakers over concerns about how it handles user data. The Chinese company says it stores data on servers in China, but officials believe it has not clearly explained how the data is used or who can access it.

The Trump administration is expected to introduce a rule blocking the download of DeepSeekГўВҖВҷs chatbot on US government devices, sources said.

Officials are also weighing two other actions. They may ban the app from US app stores and restrict US cloud service providers from offering DeepSeekГўВҖВҷs AI models. However, these discussions are still in the early stages, according to people familiar with the matter.

States and Other Countries Take Action against DeepSeek

The U.S. government has no full ban on DeepSeek, but some agencies, like the Navy and NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration), have blocked the app over security and privacy fears. New York has banned the app from state devices, warning that it could risk user privacy. Also, Texas and other states have taken steps to limit the appГўВҖВҷs use.

The U.S. is not alone in its concerns regarding DeepSeek. Countries like Australia, Canada, and South Korea have already blocked the app from their government networks.

What Comes Next for DeepSeek

The proposed ban may not stop with government devices. U.S. officials are also considering removing DeepSeek from app stores and blocking its access to American cloud services.

If the U.S. moves forward with these restrictions, DeepSeek could face major setbacks. Being removed from app stores would limit user growth and reduce its presence in one of the worldГўВҖВҷs largest tech markets.

Adding to its challenges, a recent NewsGuard audit found that DeepSeekГўВҖВҷs chatbot had only 17% accuracy, with an 83% fail rate, worse than the 62% average of Western rivals like Microsoft-backed OpenAIГўВҖВҷs ChatGPT (MSFT) and GoogleГўВҖВҷs Gemini (GOOGL). With growing scrutiny and potential bans, DeepSeekГўВҖВҷs future in the U.S. remains uncertain.

What Is the Best AI Stock to Buy?

data.title: AppleвҖҷs (AAPL) AI Struggles Deepen as Siri Upgrade Delayed Until 2026
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) has pushed back key AI upgrades for the Siri voice assistant, saying these enhancements will take ГўВҖВңlonger than we thought.ГўВҖВқ They are now expected ГўВҖВңin the coming year,ГўВҖВқ possibly extending into 2026. Meanwhile, rivals such as AlphabetГўВҖВҷs (GOOGL) Google and Amazon (AMZN) continue to advance their AI capabilities.

These upgrades, initially unveiled in June 2024, aimed to make Siri smarter and more useful by allowing it to handle more complex tasks. This includes reading on-screen content, like spotting an address in a text and saving it to contacts. Also, it would let Siri search photos or manage tasks inside apps.

AppleГўВҖВҷs AI Plans Face Delays

Despite Apple’s efforts to enhance Siri’s capabilities, many of the most awaited features remain unfinished.

Apple has not shared the exact cause for the delay, but reports say internal tests found bugs and slowdowns. These issues have raised concerns among top leaders. Fixing the issues may mean rebuilding parts of Siri, which could delay the timeline further.

At the same time, adding new AI features while keeping user data safe brings tough challenges for the company.

Rivals Race Ahead with Smarter AI Assistants

While Apple faces delays, Google and Amazon are moving fast with their AI initiatives. For instance, GoogleГўВҖВҷs Gemini AI now powers SamsungГўВҖВҷs latest phones, giving users advanced voice help. Similarly, Amazon also launched a new version of Alexa that allows smoother, more human-like chats.

These moves put pressure on Apple to speed up its own AI work. Experts say AppleГўВҖВҷs slow progress may hurt iPhone sales, as buyers want smarter tools. Still, the company plans to roll out SiriГўВҖВҷs AI in more languages next month.

As Google and Amazon push ahead, Apple must act fast to keep up in the fierce AI race.

Is Apple a Buy, Sell, or Hold?ГӮВ 

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 33 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy, 11 Hold, and four Sell recommendations assigned in the last three months. TheГӮВ average AAPL price targetГӮВ of $250.95 implies 4.97% upside from current levels.

See more AAPL analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Top Analyst Bangs the Drum on Super Micro Computer Stock
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) is a name that has courted plenty of controversy over the past year. The issue has centered on the reliability of its financials given the company delayed the filing of financial reports, which resulted in SMCI facing delisting from the NASDAQ. The disarray was such that Ernest Young resigned as its independent auditor, citing a lack of trust in management.

But the story has taken a turn. SMCI has finally submitted its overdue reports, regaining compliance with regulators ГўВҖВ“ an event that Northland analyst Nehal Chokshi sees as a game-changer. According to Chokshi, the financials have been proven to be trustable with a ГўВҖВңhigh degree of certainty.ГўВҖВқ

With that chapter closed, itГўВҖВҷs time to shift the focus to what lies ahead for the company. To gain deeper insight, Chokshi, who ranks in the top 1% of Wall Street stock pros, recently engaged with SMCIГўВҖВҷs investor relations team. So, what key takeaways did he uncover?

Well, for one, SMCI still has the same outlook for the March quarter, expecting results to range from a 12% decline to a 6% increase compared to the previous quarter. This is mainly because SMCIГўВҖВҷs GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) customers are unlikely to be among the first to receive NvidiaГўВҖВҷs Blackwell GPUs.

Chokshi also notes that the Street expects DellГўВҖВҷs servers and networking business to remain flat sequentially in the April quarter, aligning with the midpoint of SMCIГўВҖВҷs guide. This suggests that NvidiaГўВҖВҷs Blackwell shipments in the January quarter were primarily directed toward hyperscalers, which Dell and SMCI donГўВҖВҷt usually serve. The analyst also reminds investors that thereГўВҖВҷs about an eight-week delay between Nvidia recognizing revenue and when server OEMs, like Dell and SMCI, reflect it in their earnings.

Looking ahead, Chokshi sees SMCIГўВҖВҷs ambitious $40 billion revenue target for FY26 as achievable, though heГўВҖВҷs maintaining a more conservative estimate of $30.9 billion ГўВҖВ“ what he believes is the minimum the company will achieve. Meanwhile, industry checks suggest SMCI has a significant competitive edge in GenAI servers, largely due to its early adoption of Direct Liquid Cooling (DLC) technology. Additionally, its modular component design, rooted in its well-known Building Block Architecture, continues to be a key differentiator.

ГўВҖВңAs such,ГўВҖВқ the 5-star analyst went on to say, ГўВҖВңwe believe SMCI is well positioned to return to a share gain trajectory in GenAI servers minimally starting in F1Q26 (Sept Q), and as such we see SMCIГўВҖВҷs $40B FY26 target (65% revenue growth relative to FY25 midpoint guidance) as reasonable.ГўВҖВқ

All told, Chokshi rates SMCI shares as an Outperform (i.e., Buy), while raising his price target from $57 to a joint Street-high of $70, implying the stock will gain 83% in the months ahead.

Yet, not everyone on Wall Street shares his enthusiasm. When it comes to ratings, the verdict is mixed: 4 Holds, 3 Buys, and 2 Sells leave SMCI with a cautious Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating. However, the average price target sits at $49.14, pointing to a still-solid 28.5% gain over the next year. (See SMCI stock forecast)

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: вҖҳBears, Get Ready to Lose,вҖҷ Says Daniel Ives on Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is currently receiving the thumbs down from car buyers worldwide, with sales plummeting in various regions as prospective consumers distance themselves from a brand synonymous with Elon Musk.

However, that fact appears inconsequential when considering the companyГўВҖВҷs prospects. At least that is the opinion of Wedbush analyst and Tesla bull Daniel Ives.

ГўВҖВңWhile the DOGE/Trump Musk iron clad partnership has created major brand worries for Tesla…we estimate less than 5% of Tesla sales globally are at risk from these issues despite the global draconian narrative for Musk,ГўВҖВқ the analyst said.

While MuskГўВҖВҷs actions appear to be distancing the core Tesla consumer base, Ives points out that throughout the past decade, there have been several instances in its journey where negative sentiment and Street concerns have overshadowed the ГўВҖВңunique disruptive global tech story.ГўВҖВқ From the tense production challenges in 2017/2018, to financial concerns, to the Musk/Twitter drama in 2022, and now once again, Tesla bulls are with their ГўВҖВңback against the wall facing global negative sentimentГўВҖВқ due to Musk’s involvement with DOGE and the Trump administration. ГўВҖВңThis is a gut check moment for the Tesla bulls (including ourselves) after this massive sell-off in Tesla shares with fears mounting,ГўВҖВқ Ives concedes.

For now, the bears ГўВҖВңown the Tesla narrative,” and MuskГўВҖВҷs political affiliations arenГўВҖВҷt doing him any favors. But Ives remains unfazed, believing that the upcoming catalysts will trump any negative sentiment. ГўВҖВңThis is the start of the biggest innovation and technology cycle in Tesla’s history ahead over the next few years,ГўВҖВқ he confidently says, before running off a list of what will get Tesla back on track again.

First, before summer, there will be the launch of a new sub-$35k Model, which will ГўВҖВңhelp drive pent-up EV consumer demand globallyГўВҖВқ ГўВҖВ“ an interesting phrase considering how sales are tanking right now.

Then thereГўВҖВҷs the rollout of unsupervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) in Austin this June ГўВҖВ“ an event Ives sees as nothing short of transformational. In fact, he believes TeslaГўВҖВҷs autonomous push alone could be worth $1 trillion, declaring it ГўВҖВңthe biggest transformation to the auto industry in modern day historyГўВҖВқ and predicting Tesla will dominate the global autonomous market.

IvesГўВҖВҷ bullishness doesnГўВҖВҷt end there. In fact, the analyst thinks that Trump in the White House is ГўВҖВңthe best thing that ever happened to Musk and Tesla.ГўВҖВқ ThatГўВҖВҷs because it will establish a deregulatory environment with a federal autonomous roadmap ГўВҖВңcentral to the Tesla golden strategic vision.ГўВҖВқ

Bottom line, Ives sees a massive opportunity ahead. So much so that heГўВҖВҷs added TSLA to WedbushГўВҖВҷs Best Ideas List. Naturally, he rates the stock an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating and slaps a Street-high price target of $550 ГўВҖВ” a 109% upside from current levels. (To watch IvesГўВҖВҷ track record, click here)

Of course, opinions on Wall Street are split. While 13 analysts echo IvesГўВҖВҷ bullish stance, 11 remain on the sidelines with Hold ratings, and 11 are outright bearish. As a result, the consensus lands at Hold (i.e., Neutral). But even with that tempered outlook, thereГўВҖВҷs still room for gains ГўВҖВ” at $347.96, the average price target suggests a 32% climb in the months ahead. (See Tesla stock forecast)

data.title: NVIDIA GTC 2025: AI Breakthroughs, Quantum Leap, and Big Market Moves
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA) has had an underwhelming start to the year, declining 16.08% year-to-date. However, the company is dusting off and ironing its suits for next weekГўВҖВҷs grand technological occasion: the annual GPU Technology Conference (GTC) 2025, which will be held from March 17 to 21. The peak moment will occur when CEO Jensen Huang, the keynote speaker on March 18, is expected to unveil major AI and computing advancements.

With over 25,000 in-person attendees and 300,000 virtual viewers, the event is shaping up as the largest AI-focused conference of the year. Investors and industry experts will closely watch for product launches, strategic moves, and the companyГўВҖВҷs evolving role in AI, cloud computing, and quantum technology.

What Is Expected at the GTC?

ThereГўВҖВҷs great anticipation for significant reveals of the next-generation AI hardware, including potential updates to NVIDIAГўВҖВҷs Hopper and Blackwell architectures, which power AI models and data centers. The company is also making a notable push into quantum computing, hosting its first-ever Quantum Day on March 20, signaling interest in hybrid AI-quantum solutions. Additionally, major advancements are expected in CUDA, AI supercomputing, and new cloud partnerships with Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN).

The conference will feature other key players from the tech industry, such as Google (GOOGL), AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), Tesla (TSLA), and OpenAI in attendance. AI startups and enterprise tech leaders will showcase innovations across industries, from self-driving cars to robotics and healthcare.

Although NvidiaГўВҖВҷs stock has been experiencing a rough start to 2025, showcasing new and advanced products or strategic shifts at GTC could drive NVDA shares higher. If Nvidia also proves it has a genuine foothold in quantum computing, it could solidify its leadership against rivals such as IBM (IBM) or Google. With the AI revolution accelerating, GTC 2025 is becoming a pivotal event for investors, researchers, and tech enthusiasts alike.

Is NVDA a Buy, or a Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, Nvidia is considered a Strong Buy, based on 42 analysts ratings. The average price target for NVDA stock is $177.41, suggesting a 57.43% upside potential.

See more NVDA analyst ratings
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: S&P 500 Faces One of Its Worst Slumps in History вҖ“ Is TrumpвҖҷs Trade Policy to Blame?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX), the worldГўВҖВҷs leading index comprising the biggest 500 traded companies, is now lagging. In one of its worst weeks in decades, the index dropped 3.1%, erasing over $3 trillion in value since its February peak. Meanwhile, global markets have performed better, which might be signaling a shift in investor confidence. The U.S. share of global market capitalization has also fallen below 50%, marking a decline in dominance.

Several factors are driving this downturn. The U.S. economy shows signs of weakness, with declining home sales, rising unemployment claims, and slowing consumer spending. These concerns have created a solid infrastructure for investors’ hesitance, leading to market pullback. Nevertheless, the overall reaction to President Trump’s ongoing trade wars has had an adverse effect on the market and is considered a primary catalyst to the index’s recent slump.

Tariffs Uncertainty and Tech Stocks Decline

Trade policy uncertainty has added to the pressure. President TrumpГўВҖВҷs tariffs, including a 25% tariff on Canadian and Mexican goods that has now been delayed to April, have clearly unsettled markets. Also, the ongoing trade tensions with China and threats of further tariffs on other nations have only worsened investor sentiment. With global trade facing potential disruptions, companies that rely on international markets could suffer, further weighing on stock performance.

amounted to roughly $854 billion. The technology sector, a major driver of the S&P 500, has been hit hard. Giants like Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) have lost hundreds of billions in market value, dragging the index down. For example, Nvidia has lost $35 in stock value since Jan. 23, which amounted to roughly $854 billion. Tesla has shed over $160 in stock value during the same time frame, accumulating over $500 billion in losses. A staggering numbers by all accounts.

Given its heavy tech weighting, the S&P 500 has struggled more than its global counterparts that are less reliant on this sector.

Investing Elsewhere?

The question now is whether this trend will continue. Historically, when the S&P 500 falls behind early in the year, it rarely recovers to outperform global markets. U.S. stocks may struggle if economic conditions and trade tensions donГўВҖВҷt improve. This raises an important question for American investors: Should they stick with U.S. stocks or start looking at global opportunities? If the current trends persist, diversification outside the U.S. could become an increasingly attractive option.

data.author: Ran Melamed
